3.happening: Count the elements to bound the search.
3.why: We'll consider every possible start and end.

4.happening: Start the best sum at the first element.
4.why: A one-element subarray is always valid, so all-negative arrays are handled correctly.
4.invariant: max_sum is the best subarray sum so far.

5.happening: Fix the subarray start at index i.
5.why: We'll grow every subarray that begins here.

6.happening: Reset the running sum for the new start.
6.why: Each new start begins its own fresh subarray sum.
6.invariant: current_sum is the sum of the window nums[i..j], empty so far.

7.happening: Extend the subarray end to index j.
7.why: Growing one element at a time lets the sum update in O(1).

8.happening: Add nums[{j}] ({nums[j]}) → the window sum becomes {current_sum + nums[j]}.
8.why: Extending by one element just adds that element to the previous sum.
8.invariant: current_sum is the sum of nums[i..j].

9.happening: Is this window's sum ({current_sum}) a new best?
9.why: We track the largest sum across all subarrays.

10.why: This subarray beats every one tried before it.
10.invariant: max_sum is the largest subarray sum seen.

11.happening: All subarrays checked — return the best sum ({max_sum}).
11.why: Exhaustive and O(n²); Kadane reaches the same answer in O(n).
11.invariant: max_sum is the global maximum subarray sum.

init.happening: Setting up: element count and initial best.

loop.why: Every subarray begins at some start i.

update.happening: Updating the running window sum or the best.
update.why: The window sum grows as the end extends.

move.why: Only later ends form new subarrays for this start.
move.invariant: j runs from i to the end.

check.happening: Comparing this window's sum to the best.
check.why: We want the largest sum over all subarrays.

return.invariant: max_sum is the global maximum subarray sum.
